commands.review_summary:
The PR branch modifies doctor repair sequencing and plugin dependency cleanup for parallel I/O, adds a migration guard, changes JSON read retry handling, alters bundled plugin load-path repair, and adds gateway client pooling.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings by source inspection: the PR head drops cleanup guards present on current main and reuses GatewayClient instances whose callbacks are fixed at construction. I did not run runtime tests because this review is read-only.

target.title: perf(doctor): parallel I/O + guard conditions (1A+1B)
target.body:
## Phase 1A + 1B: Parallel I/O + Guard Conditions

### Performance optimization for doctor --fix

Issue: #85333

### Overview

Two complementary optimizations to reduce `openclaw doctor --fix` wall-clock time from ~229s towards ~55s:

**Phase 1A — Parallel I/O** (shared foundation)
- `repair-sequencing.ts`: 3 I/O-only repair steps (plugin dep cleanup, OAuth repairs) run concurrently via `Promise.all`
- `plugin-dependency-cleanup.ts`: directory walks parallelized — 50+ plugin scans and 6 root-level scans run concurrently

**Phase 1B — Guard Conditions** (additive on 1A)
- New `migration-guard.ts` utility with `hasCompletedMigration()` and `stampMigration()` functions
- Enables one-time migration checks to stamp `config.meta.lastMigrationVersion` on success
- Future-proof: individual checks can add early-return guards for already-completed migrations

### Why this approach

Running `doctor --fix` on every update is the expected workflow. Making checks faster (1A) + allowing them to skip when not needed (1B) means users get the same safety with less waiting.

### Verification
- 32/32 tests pass
- Code review: reviewed by deepseek-v4-flash (code-reviewer persona, 5-axis framework)
- Test coverage for migration guard utility: 11 test cases
